messages.content: You are an on-call SRE for NimbusWorks. Multiple services are alerting. Exactly one service has a real ROOT-CAUSE fault; other alerting services are only cascading symptoms of an unhealthy dependency. Use the tools to diagnose the root cause, apply the CORRECT fix for the fault type, and verify every service is healthy. Do NOT blindly restart everything: a restart does not fix a bad config, a bad deploy, or an exhausted pool. You have a limited tool-call budget.

Dashboard is red -- a bunch of services just went unhealthy. Services: courierbot, gatekeeper, glacierdocs, ledgerline, mistral-cache, pulseboard, quillbase, streamforge, tokensmith, vaultkeep.
Diagnose the root cause and restore all services to healthy. Fix types: bad_config -> set_config then restart; bad_deploy -> rollback; pool_exhausted -> set_config(pool_max>=512) then restart; dependency outage -> restart the ROOT service (not the symptom). Find the root cause and fix it.

messages.content: You are an on-call SRE for NimbusWorks. Multiple services are alerting. Exactly one service has a real ROOT-CAUSE fault; other alerting services are only cascading symptoms of an unhealthy dependency. Use the tools to diagnose the root cause, apply the CORRECT fix for the fault type, and verify every service is healthy. Do NOT blindly restart everything: a restart does not fix a bad config, a bad deploy, or an exhausted pool. You have a limited tool-call budget.

PagerDuty just lit up -- multiple NimbusWorks services are alerting. Services: courierbot, gatekeeper, glacierdocs, ledgerline, mistral-cache, pulseboard, quillbase, streamforge, tokensmith, vaultkeep.
Diagnose the root cause and restore all services to healthy. Fix types: bad_config -> set_config then restart; bad_deploy -> rollback; pool_exhausted -> set_config(pool_max>=512) then restart; dependency outage -> restart the ROOT service (not the symptom). Find the root cause and fix it.
